description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone base and a wooden frame supporting a pitched roof made of red, overlapping tiles. The well features a horizontal wooden axle with a hand crank on one side, used for drawing water, and a spindle mechanism above the well opening. The roof is supported by two vertical wooden posts, slightly angled for stability, and the entire structure sits on a roughly octagonal stone platform with scattered dirt and rocks. The stonework of the well is robust and the wood appears unfinished, giving a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The roof tiles are unevenly shaped, adding to the handcrafted aesthetic, and the well is open at the top with no visible cover. There are no visible decorative elements, and the structure is designed primarily for function and durability.
description: A rustic stone water well with a red-tiled wooden roof, hand crank, and open circular stone base, set on a stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank, spindle, and full roof with red tiles, as well as the open well mouth and stone platform.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden frame's thickness, the profile of the roof tiles, and the circular stone well structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but without the hand crank visible, showing the back of the spindle and the well opening.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the wooden support structure and the layered roof tiles, with the well's stonework visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A horizontal spindle and hand crank mechanism are present, made of wood, but no bucket or rope is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low, circular stone wall and covered by a wooden roof supported by two posts.
